StatDef.label: apparel wear rate factor
StatDef.description: A multiplier to the rate at which worn apparel deteriorates.\n\nThis only affects hit point loss from gradual deterioration, not hit point loss from damaging hits.

StatDef.label: boredom decay
StatDef.description: The amount of joy tolerance, or 'boredom', lost per day. This is in addition to the joy tolerance drop per day from one's expectation level.

StatDef.label: overdose susceptibility
StatDef.description: Multiplies the random chance that ingesting drugs causes a severe overdose, as well as the amount of guaranteed overdose severity from ingesting hard drugs.

StatDef.label: skill gain from recreation
StatDef.description: A multiplier to the skill xp gained while engaging in a joy activity.

StatDef.label: caravan visibility offset
StatDef.description: When this pawn is added to a caravan, it adds this value to the total body size of the caravaneers for the purposes of determining caravan visibility. Negative values will reduce caravan visibility; positive values will increase it.

StatDef.label: caravan visibility factor
StatDef.description: A multiplier to this pawn's body size for the purposes of determining caravan visibility.

StatDef.label: jump range factor
StatDef.description: A multiplier to the range of jump abilities used by this character.

StatDef.label: spew range factor
StatDef.description: A multiplier to the range of spray or spew abilities used by this character.

StatDef.label: track size
StatDef.description: Affects the amount of loose terrain (such as sand or dirt) this character tracks onto other surfaces.

StatDef.label: pilfering stealth
StatDef.description:
The chance to avoid being caught when attempting a pilfering activity. Pilfering activities include pickpocketing (right-click on a non-hostile pawn with an inventory to try stealing random items from it) and burglary (visit another settlement and press the Burgle button to try stealing items of a desired category from it).

For pickpocketing, success chance equals the target's alert level minus the thief's pilfering stealth; for burglary, success chance equals the settlement's alert level minus the average pilfering stealth of all free pilfering-capable colonists in the caravan. A difference of 1 or higher guarantees success, while a difference of 0 or lower guarantees failure.

A pawn must have a pilfering stealth higher than 0 in order to be capable of pilfering.

A target's alert level is shown in parentheses on the option to pilfer from it. Alert level gains a high but gradually decaying boost after being pilfered from, which can be more severe if the total market value of pilfered items is very high. The base alert level of more technologically advanced settlements is higher; the base alert level of traders is based on a number of factors, including their senses, social skill, and pilfering stealth.

StatDef.label: max pilfering value
StatDef.description: The maximum net market value of items that can be stolen during a single pilfering attempt. Pilfering activities include pickpocketing (right-click on a non-hostile pawn with an inventory to try stealing random items from it) and burglary (visit another settlement and press the Burgle button to try stealing items of a desired category from it).\n\nThe total weight of items that can be pilfered is still ultimately limited by the mass capacity of the caravan (for burglary) or the carry capacity of the thief (for pickpocketing).

StatDef.label: alert level
StatDef.description: The ability to detect and thwart pickpocketing attempts.

StatDef.label: surveying speed
StatDef.description: How quickly this person can find things using scanning equipment.

StatDef.label: breach damage factor
StatDef.description: A multiplier to the amount of damage dealt to buildings.

StatDef.label: anti-entity damage factor
StatDef.description: A multiplier to the amount of damage dealt to unnatural entities.

StatDef.label: melee armor penetration factor
StatDef.description: A multiplier on melee armor penetration.

StatDef.label: ranged armor penetration factor
StatDef.description: A multiplier on ranged armor penetration.
